seo.title: Building Voice Agents with Google ADK + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build voice agents with Google ADK + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building voice agents with Google's Agent Development Kit (ADK) enables real-time conversational AI powered by Gemini models, processing speech-to-text, LLM reasoning, and text-to-speech in sub-second pipelines. Guardrails are essential due to high-stakes real-time interactions where latency violations, incoherent responses, or safety failures can erode user trust. Vex provides runtime verification to ensure response appropriateness, latency budgets, conversation coherence, and safety filtering.

architectureOverview.heading: Voice Agents Architecture with Google ADK
architectureOverview.description:
Voice agents follow a real-time pipeline: audio input is transcribed via speech-to-text (e.g., Google Cloud Speech-to-Text), fed into ADK's multi-agent orchestration for Gemini-powered reasoning and tool delegation, then synthesized back to speech using text-to-speech (e.g., Google Cloud TTS). ADK manages agent delegation, session state, and tool calls, but long conversations risk state corruption while multi-agent handoffs can create circular dependencies or format mismatches. Sub-second latency demands optimized model switching and efficient callbacks, where Vex integrates as a before_agent_callback to verify inputs/outputs without blocking the critical path.

The architecture emphasizes stateless agents with shared session memory, real-time streaming, and fallback mechanisms for latency spikes. Common patterns include root orchestrator agents delegating to specialist sub-agents for tasks like intent classification or tool execution.

risks.name: Multi-agent delegation creating circular dependencies
risks.description: Agents endlessly delegate tasks without resolution, causing infinite loops and latency violations
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies delegation graphs before execution, blocking cycles via graph analysis

risks.name: Gemini model switching causing output inconsistency
risks.description: Dynamic model selection (e.g., Gemini 1.5 Pro vs Flash) leads to format mismatches across agents
risks.mitigation: Vex enforces consistent output schemas regardless of model selection

risks.name: Session state corruption in long conversations
risks.description: Shared memory drift causes incoherent context in extended voice interactions
risks.mitigation: Vex validates session state integrity and conversation coherence before agent calls

risks.name: Tool response format mismatches across agents
risks.description: Agents expect different JSON schemas from shared tools, breaking the pipeline
risks.mitigation: Vex normalizes tool responses to canonical formats before agent consumption

implementation.title: Basic Voice Agent Pipeline with ADK
implementation.description: Core speech-to-text → ADK → text-to-speech pipeline with real-time requirements
implementation.code:
import google.cloud.speech_v1p1beta1 as speech
from google.adk import Agent, Session, before_agent_callback
import google.cloud.texttospeech as tts

# STT client
stt_client = speech.SpeechClient()

# TTS client
tts_client = tts.TextToSpeechClient()

# ADK Voice Agent
voice_agent = Agent(
    name="voice_agent",
    model="gemini-1.5-flash",
    tools=[...],  # Your tools here
    before_agent_callback=vex_callback  # Vex integration point
)

def process_audio(audio_bytes):
    # Speech-to-Text
    audio = speech.RecognitionAudio(content=audio_bytes)
    config = speech.RecognitionConfig(
        encoding=speech.RecognitionConfig.AudioEncoding.LINEAR16,
        sample_rate_hertz=16000,
        language_code="en-US",
        enable_automatic_punctuation=True
    )
    response = stt_client.recognize(config=config, audio=audio)
    transcript = response.results[0].alternatives[0].transcript
    
    # ADK Agent Reasoning
    session = Session(user_id="voice_user")
    agent_response = voice_agent.run(transcript, session=session)
    
    # Text-to-Speech
    synthesis_input = tts.SynthesisInput(text=agent_response.text)
    voice = tts.VoiceSelectionParams(language_code="en-US", name="en-US-Wavenet-D")
    audio_config = tts.AudioConfig(audio_encoding=tts.AudioEncoding.MP3)
    tts_audio = tts_client.synthesize_speech(
        input=synthesis_input, voice=voice, audio_config=audio_config
    )
    return tts_audio.audio_content

implementation.title: Multi-Agent Orchestration Setup
implementation.description: Root orchestrator delegates to specialist agents with session state management
implementation.code:
from google.adk import Agent, MultiAgentSystem, Session

# Specialist agents
intent_agent = Agent(name="intent", model="gemini-1.5-flash")
entity_agent = Agent(name="entity", model="gemini-1.5-pro")
action_agent = Agent(name="action", model="gemini-1.5-flash")

# Root orchestrator
orchestrator = Agent(
    name="voice_orchestrator",
    model="gemini-1.5-flash",
    agents=[intent_agent, entity_agent, action_agent],
    delegation_strategy="sequential",
    before_agent_callback=vex_guardrail_callback
)

# Multi-agent system
voice_system = MultiAgentSystem(agents=[orchestrator])

def handle_conversation(transcript, session_id):
    session = Session(session_id=session_id, max_turns=50)
    response = voice_system.run(transcript, session=session)
    return response.final_output

implementation.title: Real-time Latency Monitoring
implementation.description: Wrap ADK calls with latency budget enforcement for sub-second voice requirements
implementation.code:
import time
from google.adk import Agent, Session

class LatencyGuard:
    def __init__(self, budget_ms=800):
        self.budget_ms = budget_ms
    
    def check_pipeline(self, transcript):
        start_time = time.time()
        
        # STT (mocked as ~150ms)
        stt_time = 150
        
        # ADK Agent (measured)
        agent_start = time.time()
        response = voice_agent.run(transcript)
        agent_time = (time.time() - agent_start) * 1000
        
        # TTS (mocked as ~100ms)
        tts_time = 100
        
        total_latency = stt_time + agent_time + tts_time
        
        if total_latency > self.budget_ms:
            raise TimeoutError(f"Latency violation: {total_latency}ms > {self.budget_ms}ms")
        
        return response

latency_guard = LatencyGuard(budget_ms=800)
# Integrate with main pipeline
result = latency_guard.check_pipeline(user_transcript)

implementation.description: Maintain conversation history and prevent state corruption in long sessions

vexIntegration.title: Vex before_agent_callback Setup
vexIntegration.description: Add Vex as callback to verify all agent inputs/outputs before execution
vexIntegration.code:
from vex import VexClient
from google.adk import before_agent_callback

vex_client = VexClient(api_key="your-vex-api-key")

@vex_client.guardrail(
    verifications=[
        "response_appropriateness",
        "latency_budget",
        "conversation_coherence",
        "safety_filtering"
    ],
    risk_profile="high",
    timeout_ms=750
)
def vex_voice_guardrail(ctx):
    """Vex callback for ADK voice agents"""
    
    # Verify input appropriateness
    if not ctx.input_is_safe(ctx.message):
        return "I cannot assist with that request."
    
    # Check conversation coherence
    if ctx.session_turn > 20:
        coherence_score = ctx.verify_coherence(ctx.session_history[-10:])
        if coherence_score < 0.7:
            return "Let me clarify - what specifically would you like help with?"
    
    # Latency budget check
    if ctx.latency_forecast() > 800:
        return "One moment while I process that..."
    
    # Allow agent execution
    return ctx.PROCEED

# Register with ADK
voice_agent = Agent(
    name="voice_agent",
    before_agent_callback=vex_voice_guardrail
)
